title: MCP Prompts
concept: Reusable, parameterized prompt templates a server can offer, letting servers ship curated interaction patterns.

exercises.prompt: In the last lesson, we learned about MCP Resources. What do they allow a server to do?

options.text: Expose read-only files, records, or live data to the host context
Execute system bash scripts automatically on the host machine
Write to database tables and change server state directly

exercises.explain: MCP Resources allow a server to expose read-only data, like file logs or database tables, which the client can read and add to the context window.

cards.title: Pre-Packaged AI Workflows
cards.body: Writing the perfect system prompt for every task is hard. What if your favorite external tools could hand you pre-packaged, expert-written prompt templates that just work?

cards.title: What are MCP Prompts?
cards.body: MCP Prompts are server-defined, reusable templates that clients can discover and use. They accept parameters, letting servers guide the LLM's behavior with pre-optimized instructions and context.

cards.title: The Cookbook Analogy
cards.body: Think of MCP Prompts like a cookbook provided by a master chef. Instead of trying to write a complex recipe from scratch, you just pick the 'Bake a Cake' template and fill in the flavor parameter (like 'chocolate').

cards.title: Prompts in Action
cards.body: An MCP server for code review might expose a prompt called 'analyze-security'. The client passes the 'code' parameter, and the server generates a fully-structured system prompt optimized for finding security bugs in that code.

cards.title: Prompts vs. Resources
cards.body: While both live on the server, resources are static data sources (like database logs). Prompts are active instructions that frame how the LLM should behave and respond.

cards.title: Data vs. Instructions
cards.body: Compare how raw resources differ from interactive prompt templates.

left.text: Static data or logs read directly by the LLM without formatting.

right.text: A structured instruction template waiting for inputs to guide the LLM.

exercises.prompt: What is the main benefit of MCP Prompts?

options.text: They allow the LLM to write its own server code.
They allow servers to expose pre-written prompt templates that take arguments.
They block the LLM from accessing system tools.
They are read-only documents that the LLM cannot edit.

exercises.explain: MCP Prompts are parameterized templates that the server exposes, allowing the client to request structured prompts by supplying specific inputs.

exercises.prompt: True or False: MCP Prompts can accept arguments to customize the prompt generated by the server.
exercises.explain: Yes! MCP Prompts can accept variables (like language, file path, or user query) to customize the output prompt.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how MCP Prompts are structured on the server:
exercises.sentence: Servers expose prompt templates that clients can load with custom arguments.
exercises.explain: MCP Prompts are server-defined templates that accept parameters to generate tailored system prompts for the LLM.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following interaction logs represents an MCP server serving a parameterized prompt template?

options.text: User: Please review this code for bugs: print('hello world')
User: Can you check if there are any SQL injection issues in this query?
AI Server: Template 'analyze-code' generated prompt: 'Identify security issues in this code: SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = 1'

exercises.explain: Option C shows a clear, automated template rendering prompt ('Identify security issues in this code:') utilizing a dynamic parameter ('{{code}}') typical of an MCP prompt server, whereas A and B represent manual user inputs.

exercises.prompt: You want to build an MCP server that helps users translate code between programming languages. Which of the following best describes how you would implement this using MCP Prompts?

options.text: A prompt template hosted on the server that takes arguments to generate a structured system prompt
A client-side hardcoded system prompt that cannot accept dynamic parameters
An automated agent that writes system prompts using reinforcement learning
A read-only database query that bypasses the LLM entirely

exercises.explain: MCP Prompts are pre-packaged, parameterized templates stored on the server side. They allow developers to bundle complex, structured prompts and let clients easily use them by just filling in the blank parameters (like 'language' or 'code_snippet').
